description: Pulmonary embolism with NORMAL SpO2. Atypical presentation: only mild tachycardia and pleuritic chest discomfort.

patient.name: Karen S., 38F
patient.chief_complaint: Sharp right-sided chest discomfort worse with deep breath for 6 hours. Mild shortness of breath but feels mostly OK at rest. Calf has been mildly sore for 3 days, she thought from new running shoes.
patient.history: Otherwise healthy, on combined oral contraceptives, recent 8-hour flight from Europe 4 days ago, no recent surgery

rubric_notes: **CRITICAL MISS TEST.** Vitals look benign (SpO2 96 NORMAL, BP normal, only HR 108 mildly tachy). NO classic 'severe hypoxia' presentation. BUT: 38-year-old on OCPs + recent long flight + unilateral calf pain (DVT) + pleuritic chest pain + tachycardia = HIGH WELLS SCORE → PULMONARY EMBOLISM until proven otherwise. ~30% of PE patients have NORMAL SpO2 and modest tachycardia is the only abnormality. Surface signs are deceptive. Agent that misses Wells score risk factors and says ESI 3-4 because 'SpO2 fine' has missed a PE. D-dimer immediately, CT-PA if positive. PE is in the top 10 ED killers and is famously under-triaged.
